series_title: Licensing and Surveillance (Fee Bills for Fruit and Vegitables)
series_description: Fee bills for fruit and vegetables.
retention_statement: Paper: Four year retention period, provided audited by the state auditor

series_title: GAF-44 Federal or State Audit Reports
series_description: Copies of audit reports issued by the Auditor of State or federal government.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years

series_description: Used to order supplies and/or services and may be used to encumber funds for a purchase from another state agency.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years

series_description: (Continuation Sheet) Contination sheet for add- itional purchases or contract encumbrance.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years

series_description: Documentation generated for IACP review, planning, and assessments, includes IOC's, narratives, flow charts, checklists, surveys, reports, bulletins, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 2 years

series_description: Copies of violation notice letters and return responses on age and reporting violations for Brucellosis requirements under 941 and OAC 901:1-5-02.
retention_statement: Paper: 2 years retention

series_description: Information on testing and size of active herds identified with Johne's and or Testing for Johne's Disease.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned and verified as readable. ; Electronic: Retain for 3 years.

series_description: Tuberculosis testing reports from accredited veterinarians, submitted to Animal Industry as required by USDA APHIS.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office files for 3 years, disposing of fourth year as waste paper.

series_description: Records series of ID tags issued from Tag Manufacturer to Veterinarians or producers.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for five years

Records include bonding information, inspections, license applications, suspensions, and status.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 3 years.

series_description: Includes records on licenses of individuals or establishments operating weighing facilities use for, or in connection with, or incident to, the purchase or sale of livestock. Records include applications, licenses, inspections, suspensions, and status.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 2 years.

series_description: Documents authorizing movement of animals and fish coming into Ohio.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned and verified as readable. ; Electronic: Retain for 2 years.

series_title: Licensing and Surveillance (Certificates for Commercial Inspection)
series_description: Certificates for Commercial Inapection.
retention_statement: Paper: Three year retention period

series_description: Records of indemnity payments to livestock producers pursuant to
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released.

series_description: Investigative case reports on referred tissue drug residue violations prusuant to US FDA Cooperative Contract.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years, or until audited by State Auditor and US FDA.

series_description: Inspection for institution.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year after the end of the federal fiscal year in which created, provided audited by the State Audito

series_description: Schedules, confirmation of attendance, and for grades of veterinarians and senior veterinary students receiving training on Ohio Disease Control Program requirements and laboratory services utilization for the purpose of receiving federal accreditation st
retention_statement: Paper: 2 years retention

series_description: Information and test results in herds qualified and validated as free from PRV.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned and verified as readable. ; Electronic: Retain for 3 years.

series_description: Records of Inspection and Treatment of Swine at Auction Markets.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area for 6 months

series_title: GAF-84 Revenue Receipts and Holding Account Redistribution
series_description: Revenue Receipts and Holding Account Redistribution. Used to deposit and record revenue receipts from any known revenue source and to hold unknown receipts for redistribution. Also used by agencies which have established a departmental holding fund from which revenue is redistributed or by any agency which receives revenue from an unknown source and deposits it to a fund using the revenue source hold.
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy Paper after verification of scanned document is readable. ; Electronic: Retain 4 years

series_description: An order for goods and services by a state agency from a specified outside vendor or seller (other than another state agency). It represents an obligation to pay for goods or services and formally encum- bers the funds to do so.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years

series_description: Used to establish descriptive information about a vendor or update previously entered vendor data.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 4 years

series_description: Records on individuals or establishments licensed to feed garbage to swine or poultry as established under ORC 942. Records include applications, licenses, inspections, suspensions, and status.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 2 years.

series_description: USDA APHIS VS reports on cooperative state-federal Disease Programs. (Brucellosis, Tuberculosis, PRV)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area files four years, disposing of fifth year as waste paper.

series_description: Official Health Certificates completed and submitted by veterinary practitioners, verifying health of an animal moving in commerce, of for exhibition.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned and verified as readable. ; Electronic: Retain for 5 years.

series_description: Information and test results on herds certified and accredited free from Brucellosis and Tuberculosis.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 3 years. ; Paper: Retain until scanned and verified as readable.

series_description: Letter of permission to dispose of animal carcasses and parts, issued to Licensed Slaughter Establishments to document compliance with ORC 953 and ORC 918.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 3 years.

series_description: Approval letters and other related correspondence on requests from fair board for approval of veterinarians pursuant to OAC 901:1-18
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for current year, disposing of past year as waste paper, upon new year request.

series_description: Certificates submitted by practicing veterinarians to record vaccination of bovine animals against Brucellosis.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area for 3 years. Move to Agency Records Center for 4 years

series_description: Cases that have gone to appeal court.
Capital Asset Records
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years after asset is sold

series_description: Records on individuals or coorporations licensed to dispose of, pick up, render, or collect raw rendering material or transport such pursuant to ORC 953. Records include applications, inspections, licenses, suspensions, and status.
retention_statement: Paper: 4 year retention in office with disposal of the 5th year upon annual licensing. Dispose as Waste paper. Assuring audit by State Auditor.

series_description: Records contain approvals of requests, or denials to market veterinary biologic drugs pursuant to
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area for ten years

series_title: GAF-56 Maintenance and Repair of Buildin
series_description: Maintenance and repair of building.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years

series_description: Information on Livestock Dealers no longer engaged in the business of buying and selling livestock.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 4 years after inactive date.

series_description: These are reports from pesticide dealers that are sent to the Dept. of Agriculture annually. The reports show who the dealers sold pesticides to and how much they sold. Buyers of the pesticides must be licensed to use them.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 years

series_description: Results of samples taken from Apiary locations.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 2 years

series_title: Syrups And Extracts License And Applications
series_description: License and applications for syrups and extracts along with inspection and lab reports for these licenses.
retention_statement: Paper: Two year retention

series_title: Cold Storage Warehouse License And Application
series_description: License and application for Cold Storage Warehouse along with inspection and lab reports for these licenses.
retention_statement: Paper: Two year retention

series_description: The Pesticides Enforcement section of the Department of Agriculture does an investigation when a consumer calls the agency with a complaint involving a possible missuse of a pesticide
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 3 years

series_description: These are routine inspections of pesticide producers, dealers, and users by Department of Agriculture personnel.The frequency of the inspections at a given location depends on what problems, if any, are found.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 3 years

series_description: Authorization to manufacture carcass brands that are the property of the slaughter plant but kept under Meat Inspection’s control.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain as long as the slaughter plant is in operation.

series_description: This form must be completed by all inspection personnel entering slaughter establishments.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years

series_description: Meat inspectors place reject tags on facilities or equipment, for example, equipment must be re-washed or repaired. Inspectors place retai (Hold) tags on food products that need further evaluation to determine if they meet Dept. of Agriculture standards.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 3 years or until the problem has been resolved

series_description: Record of employee training and development.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 3 years after employee leaves employment.

series_description: This certificate is issued to the tester after they have passed a test showing that they are qualified to check grain deposits for type, amount, and quality/condition
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 3 years

series_description: The easement file contains the approved application, subordination agreement (if applicable), appraisal for easements (if applicable), title insurance commitment and policy, and a copy of the deed. The original recorded deed is sent to the Legal Department of the Auditor of State and then to State Archives for storage.
retention_statement: Paper: Permanent or until scanned. Deeds to be transferred to State . ; Electronic: Permanent.

series_description: Applications and materials for the Agricultural Easement Program that are not funded by the Department of Agriculture or have dropped from the purchase process.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year ; Electronic: Keep 1 year

series_description: Marketing material used to promote the Agricultural Easement Program.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year

series_description: Inspections of amusement rides that include, but are not limited to, information on location, installation, structural integrity, electrical safety, and any notice of violations issued as a result of the inspection.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep for 3 years after the year of the inspection.

series_description: The Enforcement department does accident investigations and produces a report. Enforcement keeps the original accident report and sends Ride Safety a copy. Ride Safety produces a file by adding to their copy of the accident report their ride inspections, correspondence, and notes.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep for 10 years after the date of the accident ; Paper: Retain until scanned and verified as readable.

series_description: Labels of pet food manufactured, sold, or distributed in the state of Ohio.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain the current example of the label on file until the firm goes out of business

series_description: These records include legal documents, Department of Agriculture correspondence to Scotts, information on sales from Scotts to fertilizer dealers, inspection reports describing violations, and agency internal memos about problems.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 3 years after the issue is resolved.

series_description: These records are inspections of anhydrous ammonia facilities. These facilities are inspected once per year.
retention_statement: Paper: If the report shows no problems, keep the report for 4 years. If the report cotains a problem, keep the report in the company folder until the problem is resolved.

series_description: This is an annual publication of feed, fertilizer, and lime samples tested.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep one copy for each year for 5 years. All of the other books will be distributed each year or ,if they are excess copies, they will be disposed of as waste paper.

series_description: These records include test reports and test data for evaluations performed in the National Type Evaluation Program (NTEP) lab to certify a scale, scanning device, or measuring device submitted by a private manufacturer. Records are maintained by control number and company name. The NTEP lab verifies that a weighing or measuring device meets uniform national requirements before it is approved to be utilized in the retail marketplace.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep 2 years ; Paper: Keep 5 years

series_title: County Training & Registered Service Program Records
series_description: The Registered Service Program includes both Registered Servicepersons, as well as, Registered Service Agencies. Documents associated with both categories include but are not limited to: applications, placed-in-service forms, equipment calibration certificates, test reports, payment receipts, and exam documents.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep 5 years. ; Paper: Keep 5 years.

series_description: These are records that document that weighing devices in different counties (called jurisdictions) are calibrated correctly.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep 10 years after the end of the year they were created

series_title: Correspondence With Weights And Measures Officials
series_description: These are letters from the Weights and Measures division to an Auditor, Sealer, or Inspector advising them of a weight/measure problem. A Sealer is a person in a city responsible for weights and measures. An example would be a letter to a Sealer advising them that a concrete producer in their city was producing 40 pound bags of concrete that weighed less than 40 pounds.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep 2 years unless the record is needed for future use.

series_title: Annual Listing Of Dealers And Nurseries
series_description: The division produces several hundred copies of this each year. One copy goes to each county agent in Ohio (Cooperative Extension Agent), and trade groups get a copy (i.e. Ohio Nursery and Landscape Organization). One copy also goes to each state''s Plant Pest Control department.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 copy of each year''s book for 5 years.

series_description: This is an Access database that contains information about large poultry and livestock facilities. The information includes, for example, the facility address, the names and addresses of the owner and manager, inspection reports (included as a Word attachment), animal type, and the date the permit was issued.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep 5 years.

series_description: These are licenses issued to agricultural commodity handlers, i.e. grain elevators. The file for each handler includes information relating to the licensing process, financial statements containing information ODA must keep confidential, and grain examinations for grading requirements.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 4 years after the year when the license was issued.

series_description: This certificate is issued to the tester after they have passed a test showing that they are qualified to check grain deposits for type, amount, and quality/conditon.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 3 years

series_description: These records relate to the Farmer''s Market Nutrition Program (FMNP).The Ohio Depatment of Health and the Department of Agriculture agreed to promote understanding of the FMNP, and the ODA recruited farmers to accept FMNP coupons for produce they sold at farmer''s markets.("WIC" is a program for women, infants, and children.) The Ohio Dept. of Agriculture is no longer involved with the WIC/Farmer''s Market Nutrition Program.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 5 years

series_description: These files contain research materials and reports about expanding the meat packing business in Ohio.
retention_statement: Paper: Review the files every three (3) years and dispose of documents that are no longer needed.

series_description: This file contains materials from the Center For Business & Economic Research, the Ohio Proud Telephone Survey, other marketing surveys, and information about Ohio Proud activities, promotions, and advertising. The Ohio Proud program promotes Ohio agricultural products.
retention_statement: Paper: Review partner files annually. Separate those partner files that are no longer active in the program and dispose of those that do not renew in the program after 1 year. ; Electronic: Review partner files annually. Separate those partner files that are no longer active in the program and dispose of those that do not renew in the program after 1 year.

series_description: These files are used to sell Ohio agricultural products in other countries. These files labeled, for example, "Southeast Asia" and "Western Europe" contain periodicals (magazines, trade journals, etc.) with information on food consumption patterns in different countries and different regions, e.g. the preference for rice over other foods by people in Singapore.
retention_statement: Paper: Review the files every 3 years and dispose of documents that are no longer needed.

series_description: These records include, for example, The Heartland Cuisine file that includes schedules of events for the program at the Ohio State Fair. The purpose of the Heartland Cuisine Program is promoting Ohio agricultural products.
retention_statement: Paper: Review the files every 5 years and dispose of documents that are no longer needed.

series_description: These files contain the "Market Cultivator" newsletter about trade/ promotional trips taken by Markets division personnel. The division creates the newsletter four (4) times a year and distributes it via E-Mail.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain files on a CD for 5 years.

series_description: These are the "Market Cultivator" newsletters about trade/promotional trips taken by Markets division personnel. These are paper records of past newsletters. The Markets division now distributes the newsletter by E-Mail.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 5 years

series_description: These files include, for example, the "American Food and Agriculture" file that contains a copy of the "American Food and Agriculture" magazine that was sent to the Markets division. This magazine promotes American agricultural products.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 3 years

series_description: Each file relates to one company. Each file has 3 folders: International, Ohio Proud, and General. The Ohio Proud folder contains the license application, and the General folder contains, for example, newspaper articles about the firm. These are companies that both parts (the International focus and the Ohio Proud or domestic focus) of the Markets division works with. The company files do not contain any financial statements.
retention_statement: Paper: Review files every 3 years and dispose of documents that are no longer needed.

series_description: These are files on, for example, the Ohio Beef Council. The file includes information on the directors of the Ohio Beef Council, financial statements, promotional material the council sends to the Markets division, and the council''s policies and procedures manual. Other commodity groups Markets oversees are the Apple Marketing program, Ohio Vegetable & Small Fruit Research and Development, the Ohio Corn Marketing Program, and the Ohio Egg Marketing Program.
retention_statement: Paper: Review files every 3 years and dispose of documents in them that are no longer needed.

series_description: These records include annual division IACP questionnaires, IACP training class schedules, training class completion certificates, planning documents, line manager documentation of transaction cycles, documentation of testing, memos and E-mails to line managers, and improvement plans.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 4 years

series_description: These are expenditure documents that authorize (approve)payment to a vendor (other than a state agency) for goods or services. (Employee Expense Reimbursement Requests can be attached to vouchers. These reimbursement requests have Dept. of Agriculture employee names, addresses, and social security numbers on them. This information should be shredded to prevent identity theft).
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 4 years provided audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: These are investigations of agency rules, administrative rules, or the Ohio Revised Code for divisions that include, for example, Plant Industries (e.g. incorrect, illegal use of pesticides), Amusement Ride Safety (e.g. accidents on amusement rides), Meat Inspection, Dairy, and Auctioneers. These records include, but are not limited to, case transcripts, photographs, video recordings, audio recordings, and other court documents.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep for 5 years after the case is closed. The records need to be held until all matters in the case have been resolved and all appeals have been exhausted. ; Paper: Retain paper file during open, active investigation. Once the case becomes dormant or closed, transfer to electronic storage.

series_description: These are internal agency investigations of situations involving Dept. of Agricuture employees. These investigations may involve violations of agency work rules or violations of administrative rules or the Ohio Revised Code.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 5 years after the case is closed. The records need to be kept until all matters in the case have been resolved.

series_description: These records are on microfiche sent from the Department of Commerce that used to administer the Auctioneer program. These records contain license applications and renewal applications,Auction School information (e.g. transcripts of the course of study in professional auctioneering), criminal background investigations, and miscellaneous correspondence (e.g. address changes, change of sponsors, and bond cancelations).
retention_statement: Microform: Keep for 5 years.

series_description: The records contain license applications (new and renewal), Auction School information (e.g. transcripts of the course of study in professional auctioneering and class Completion Certificates), police background investigations, and miscellaneous correspondence (e.g. address changes, changes of sponsor, and bond cancelations), and the auctioneer test answer sheet. The auctioneer test is administered at the Dept. of Agriculture.Note: The license applications contain names, addresses, and Soc. Sec. numbers.
retention_statement: Paper: Review the auctioneer files every 5 years and dispose of any that are not in active status.

series_description: Voyager (gasoline credit card company) produces this report monthly. It shows by vehicle the dates gasoline was purchased, where it was purchased, the kind of fuel purchased (diesel, unleaded, unleaded plus, etc.), the number of gallons purchased, and the price per gallon.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year

series_description: This is a report generated from Central Office during the district review that shows deficiencies at slaughter plants.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 3 years.

series_description: These records are inspections for BSE (Bovine Spongiform Encelathy) and medicated feeds for the federal government.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 1 year after the date of the inspection.

series_description: These are agency building blueprints that are "infrastructure records" as defined by
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 5 years after the structure has either been razed or the State disposes of the property.

series_description: These are reviews of county and independent fairs that were conducted by the Department of Agriculture. The records include reviews through December, 2001. The State Auditor now audits these fairs.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year

series_description: The are invoices generated by the Division of Meat Inspection which charge plant owners for time meat inspectors work in their plant beyond the normal work week (e.g. holidays and weekends).
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 3 years or until both the State audit and USDA (Federal) audit have been completed and all discrepancies are resolved

series_title: MI - 8 Certification of Exemption From Meat Inspec
series_description: This is a form completed by a retail dealer, retail butcher, restaurant, or meat processor that certifies the business is exempt from the requirements of the Meat Inspection Act.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 3 years.

series_description: Section: Grape Industries These records contain correspondence, memoranda, copies of contracts (original contracts are kept in the Legal Dept.), publications , and fiscal information relating to the work and activities of the Ohio Grape Industries Committee advertising program for Ohio wines.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep five (5) years.

series_description: Section: Grape Industries These records contain correspondence, memoranda, copies of contracts (original contracts are kept in the Legal Dept.), publications, and fiscal information relating to the research on Ohio grapes and wine conducted for the Ohio Grape Industries Committee.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep five (5) years.

series_description: Section: Grape Industries These records contain correspondence, memoranda, copies of contracts (original contracts are filed in Legal Dept.), publications and fiscal information relating to activities of the Ohio Grape Industries Committee collaborating with other organizations and agencies to provide special events to promote and market Ohio wine.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 5 years.

series_description: Section: Grape Industries These records contain correspondence, memoranda, agreements, fiscal documents, and copies oflegal documents relating to the activities of the Ohio Wine Producers Asssoc. in conjunction with the marketing and promotion mandates of the Ohio Grape Industries Committee.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years

series_description: Section: Grape Industries These records contain information about an investigation of a Department of Agriculture employee by the Enforcement Division and a Special Audit in Grape Industries for the period July 1, 1996 through March 15, 2000. The records also contain documents pertaining to contract compliance, mismanagement of public funds, and efforts to recover funds.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until until Finding for Recovery is satisfied

series_description: This is a form completed when a pool car is used. Fields on the form that are completed include Requested Time Out, Driver''s Name, Destination, Purpose, Vehicle License No., and Beginning and Ending Mileage.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year

series_description: These are books produced annually by county and independent fairs. The books contain, for example, the by-laws, schedules of events, and advertisements.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year

series_description: Section: Information Technology These are computer files of scanned travel receipts and scanned invoices stored on the Information Technology Section server.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep until audited by Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: This schedule is for ALL DIVISIONS AND ALL SECTIONS. These are paper receipts for travel expenses incurred by agency personnel.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep until audited by Auditor of State and the audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: This record lists what the label is submitted for; sketch approval, printer proof approval, final approval, type of labeling being submitted, or product. The label needs to be approved before being placed on the product. The label may contain confidential information, for example, "secret ingredients" or trade secrets.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 5 years after the product is no longer made

series_description: This is a product processing report listing ingredients and procedures used.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 5 years after product is no longer made.

series_description: This is a breakdown of the ingredients in products.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 5 years after the product is no longer made.

series_description: This is a checklist done by meat inspection at a plant. It assures that the plant has the required documents and marks of inspection,and it has sections for checking building construction, plumbing, safety, equipment, antemortem, postmortem, and processing.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 3 years.

series_description: This is a meat inspector''s review of exempt plant facilities.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 3 years

series_description: This is an evaluation form used by meat inspection to pre-approve licensing of plants.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 3 years.

series_description: This is a talley of defects found in poultry lots at slaughter plants.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 1 year in files at the plant

series_title: Weekly Itinerary For District Vets and District Su
series_description: This record is a weekly itinerary for meat inspection veterinarians and supervisors listing dates, times, and locations.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 6 months from the date shown on the form

series_description: Reports on establishments inspected.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 4 years. ; Electronic: Keep for 4 years.

series_description: Records documenting vaccination of herds/animals against Johne's Disease.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 4 years

series_description: Copies of certified letters to veterinarians on proper procedures for submitting laboratory samples.
retention_statement: Paper: Two year retention

series_description: These are completed claims for identification of livestock losses from coyotes (pursuant to ORC 955) and supporting documentation for each claim. The claim forms verify and document that required standards are met in order to receive indemnification for livestock and poultry losses caused by coyotes.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 1 year.

series_description: These records document emergency management information and procedures that relate to the Ohio Department of Agriculture Animal Disease Incident Annex, the Ohio Emergency Management Plan, and homeland security.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 10 years

series_description: These records include approval letters and related correspondence on requests from preview shows and exhibitions pursuant to the Ohio Administrative Code 901: 1-18. The records also contain custody forms and results from testing animals at each preview show/exhibition. Some files may contain investigation records that can be referred to several years later.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 5 years.

series_description: These are records documenting an individual''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''s claim for money from the Tobacco Settlement Trust. The claims have a Certification Form, Quota Owners and Sharecroppers information, and an Acknowledgement and Certification. The claims contain names, addresses, and social security numbers, and the quota information is also confidential.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 8 years.

series_description: An enforcement is an action initiated by the Dept. of Agriculture regarding a problem with pesticides (i.e.missuse of pesticide products, deficient records, etc.) Examples of enforcements are warnings, letters, license suspensions or revocations, and civil penalties
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 5 years

series_description: Credit Card payments to Pesticide and Fertilizer Regulation. All documents relating to credit card payments such as but not limited to applications, licenses, tonnage, certificates, registrations, enforcedment penalties, etc. Credit card numbers must be redacted on all credit card documents either by programming or mechanically.
retention_statement: Electronic: 3 years ; Paper: Verify and destroy immediately

series_description: These are soil analysis tests done by independent labs. The large poultry and livestock facilities send these tests results to the Ohio Department of Agriculture.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 5 years

series_description: These are inspections of large poultry and livestock facilities by the division field personnel because a complaint was made about some environmental issue like manure runoff, smells, flies, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 5 years unless a licensee or permittee is involved in litigation with the division. In this case, keep for two years after any administrative, civil, or criminal action is complete

series_description: This program registers pesticide products, specialty fertilizers and agricultural additives. All documents related to product registration such as but not limited to applications, certificates, and labels.
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy after verification ; Electronic: 3 years

series_description: This is a licensing program for people and companies. All documents relating to people or companies such as but not limited to applications and renewals, licenses, recertification forms, exam forms, certificate of insurance, tonnage restricted use dealer reports, deposit paperwork, etc. are used in this program.
retention_statement: Electronic: 3 years ; Paper: destroy after verification.

series_description: Forms used by a person/organization to enter into contract with an agency to request payment for services rendered or to amend contract. May include attendance forms, correspondence, etc.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office for seven years from date of expiration. ; Paper: Retain in office for one year from date of expiration

series_description: Documents pertaining to public requests, including but not limited to the original request, correspondence between staff and requestor or other staff, redacted and un-redacted responsive records. Maintained to document compliance with public records law obligations (ORC) 149.43.
retention_statement: Electronic: 3 years from date of request being fulfilled ; Paper: 3 years from date of request being fulfilled

series_description: Documents for licensing of meat and poultry slaughter plants, including application, license, and correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 3 years. ; Paper: Retain for 3 years, or until scanned electronically.

series_title: MI-39 Custom Slaughter and Processing Records
series_description: All custom animals for slaughter and all custom carcasses or parts for processing that do not bear the official State of Ohio or the official USDA mark of inspection must be identified at all times and must be stamped "NOT FOR SALE". The information described on this form must be maintained in a current status and kept on file at the extablishment.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep in plant files 3 years from issue date. .

series_title: MI 39 Poultry Custom Slaughter and Processing Rec
series_description: All custom birds for slaughter and all custom poultry or parts for processing that do not bear the official State of Ohio or the official U.S.D.A. inspection legend must be identified at all times. Information on this form must be maintained in a current status and kept on file at the establishment and must be available to representatives of the Ohio Department of Agriculture and the U. S. Department of Agriculture.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep in files at plant for 3 years from issue date.

series_description: Thia record is used to establish the ownership, age, and condition of an animal in Custom plants for BSE release. ("BSE" is bovine spongiform encephalopathy. BSE is a degenerative brain disease occurring in cattle).
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 3 years.

series_title: Draft Records Retention Schedule for PCP (Planned Compliance Program) and Random Reviews
series_description: Draft Records Retention Schedule for PCP (Planned Compliance Program) and Random Reviews
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 3 years from initial date of creation ; Electronic: Keep for 3 years from initial date of creation

series_description: Analysis of potential or proposed legislation and its impact on the Ohio Department of Agriculture and its stakeholders. Can include, but is not limited to, copies of current and pending legislation or draft legislation, analysis of issues and impact, correspondence, research materials, and memo recommending office position on legislation.
retention_statement: Electronic: 3 years (if maintained electronically) ; Paper: 3 years (if not maintained electronically)

series_description: Court case files serve as the primary source of litigation information and may contain, but are not limited to, pleadings, memorandum, affidavits, attorney work product, legal research, motions, judgments, discovery, evidence, transcripts, correspondence, settlement and negotiation documents, and hearing and deposition materials. Sometimes case files originate as appeals against administrative hearing rulings by client agencies.
retention_statement: Electronic: 2 years from date of final appealable order or dismissal. ; Paper: 2 years from date of final appealable order or dismissal, or until saved electronically

series_description: Administrative Hearing Files may include, but are not limited to, notice of opportunity for hearing, letters of warning / instruction, request for hearing, hearing notifications, scheduling information, case summons, court record, continuances, file sent by client (gathered during investigation), legal research, evidentiary materials, pleadings, exhibits, correspondence, attorney work product, report and recommendation from hearing officer, settlement and negotiation documents, and final adjudication order. (Includes 119 hearings).
retention_statement: Paper: 2 years until after date of final appealable order, settlement agreement, dismissal, or until saved electronically. ; Electronic: 2 years until after date of final appealable order, settlement agreement, or dismissal.

series_title: Auctioneer Program Initial License Applications and Exams
series_description: License Applications (e.g. Auctioneer; Apprentice Auctioneer; Auction Firm; Auctioneer: Corporation, LLC, Partnership, & Association; and Special Auctioneer) received under Chapter 4707 may include, but are not limited to, police background check application form, bond or irrevocable letter of credit, school certification, Ohio Secretary of State registration and certification, and payment type. Exam records include exam rosters, exams, answer sheets, verbal score sheets, and answer keys.
retention_statement: Electronic: 5 years after application is received. ; Paper: 5 years after application is received.

series_title: Auctioneer Program License Renewal and Change of Status Notifications
series_description: Renewal License Applications (e.g. Auctioneer, Apprentice Auctioneer, Auction Firm, Auctioneer: Corporation, LLC, Partnership, & Association; and Special Auctioneer) received under chapter 4707 may include but are not limited to police background check, application form, bond or irrevocable letter of credit, Ohio secretary of State certification, and notifications of change in license status (e.g. change of: name, sponsor, firm manager, address, license on deposit, and change or addition of dba).
retention_statement: Electronic: 3 years after application or status change notification is received. ; Paper: 3 years after application or status change notification is received.

series_description: Electronic database and license program record may include but is not limited to, name, SSN, date of birth, address, licensure date, license number, effective date, expiration date, date of renewal, license status, fees paid, bonding information, disciplinary actions, auction school, phone number, corporate officers or members of the LLC, license type, current license, email and web address.
retention_statement: Electronic: 5 years after license goes inactive

series_description: Records include but are not limited do, non-investigation related correspondence with public responding to general inquiries, bond cancellations, letters of instruction for ad compliance, complaints received but not investigated due to lack of jurisdiction or a lack of prima facie evidence of a violation of ORC Chapter 4707, and referrals to other agencies.
retention_statement: Electronic: 1 year after received ; Paper: 1 year after received

series_title: Auctioneer Program Investigations, Administrative Hearings, and Criminal and Civil Court Records
series_description: The records contain the agency''s investigations of potential violations of administrative rules or the ORC for the auctioneer program. The records may include but are not limited to, case investigative summaries, initial complaint, referral form, depositions, notices of opportunity of hearing letters, letters of instruction, administrative or criminal hearing notices, settlement agreements, hearing officer''s report and recommendations, hearing transcripts, recovery fund claims, and director''s orders.
retention_statement: Electronic: 5 years after final adjudication ; Paper: 5 years after final adjudication

series_title: Ohio Auctioneer Commission Auction Education Fund
series_description: The records include but are not limited to, requests for education funds, the commission''s contract, invoices, seminar mailings, sign in sheets, and certificates.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years ; Paper: Retain 2 years

series_title: Ohio Auctioneer Commission Auction School Approvals
series_description: Records include initial auction school requests and requests for re-approval received by the Ohio Auctioneer Commission, including but not limited to, correspondence, police back ground checks, curriculum, textbooks, certificates, and application letter.
retention_statement: Electronic: 4 years after application is approved or denied ; Paper: 4 years after application is approved or denied

series_description: USALIMS (Laboratory Information Management System) Reports, the associated Sample Submission Forms and any other information submitted by the clients.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years ; Electronic: 5 years

series_description: All documents and correspondence relating to application and grant or denial of wildlife shelter permit.

series_description: All documents and correspondence relating to application and grant or denial of rescue facility permit.

series_description: All documents and correspondence relating to application and grant or denial of restricted snake possession permit.

series_description: All documents and correspondence relating to application and grant or denial of restricted snake propagation permit.

series_title: Dangerous Wild Animal or Restricted Snake Relinquishment Form
series_description: Form used to voluntarily surrender ownership of dangerous wild animal or restricted snake to Ohio Department of Agriculture.

retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 6 years after transport date. ; Paper: Retain until scanned and verified as readable.

series_description: Record contains animal accession number assigned by ODA, time and date of feeding and cleaning, food and enrichment provided to animal.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 1 year. ; Paper: Retain until scanned and verified as readable.

series_title: Dangerous Wild Animal Complaint Information Form
series_description: Record contains public complaint regarding suspected dangerous wild animal violation reported to ODA Dangerous Wild Animal division.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 2 years after complaint resolved.

series_description: This record series pertains to all records that are maintained through the Ohio ALB Cooperative Eradication Program.

series_description: Complaints received electronically pertaining to High Volume Commercial Dog Breeders, Retailers & Rescues.

series_description: Application for Dog Rescue Registration.

series_description: Applications,License, State & Federal Background Checks, Insurance or Bond Certificates, Affidavits, Photo's, Background Reimbursement Forms & Proof of Receipt, Correspondence.

series_title: High Volume Commercial Dog Breeder Outreach Correspondence

retention_statement: Electronic: 2 years

series_description: This form is completed upon receipt of an animal to Dangerous Wild Animal Facility. Information includes animal diet, behavioral history, social history, medical history, surgical history, and reproductive history.

series_description: A written account of animal identification, drug and dosage administered, procedures undertaken, and observations made during anesthesia.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned and verified as readable. ; Electronic: Retain for 1 year after date of last examination.

retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned and verified as readable. ; Electronic: Retain for 1 year after date of last examination.

series_description: Record contains pathological report of examination of animal after death.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned and verified as readable. ; Electronic: Retain for 1 year after date of last examination.

series_description: Records/ Forms documenting the testing of samples submitted to CPL from state programs including Meat Inspection, Food Safety, Plant Health, Dairy, Fair, Micro EPA Water Program (any program not specified otherwise by a specific program). This includes lab records from subcontractors, chain of custody paperwork, raw data, obsolete Certificates of Analysis (COA''s) of Quality Critical Supplies, obsolete EXD''s related to procedures, forms, package inserts. Also included are equipment monitoring records and equipment calibration records (not specified otherwise by a specific program). The Section Supervisor is responsible for the record retention.

series_description: Pesticide Data Program (PDP): Sample data packages, method validation data packages and proficiency testing data packages. PDP Supervisor is the Record Retention Coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 2 years

series_description: These are Microbiology and Pesticide formulation laboratory test results and quality control records for samples associated with APP (US EPA program). Micro Supervisor is the Record Retention Coordinator. Program ended in 2012.

series_title: General Chemistry- Laboratory Drinking Water Test Records
series_description: These are Chemistry and Pesticide Residue Laboratory test records regarding drinking water samples. General Chemistry Supervisor is the Record Retention Coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 10 years. ; Electronic: Keep 10 years.

series_title: General Chemistry Laboratory Water Tests Records (Copper & Lead)
series_description: These are bench sheets (forms of test results prepared by lab personnel) of copper and lead laboratory test results on drinking water samples. General Chemistry Supervisor is the Record Retention Coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 12 years. ; Electronic: Keep 12 years.

series_title: General Chemistry- Environmental Lead Program Test Records
series_description: These are Chemistry laboratory test records regarding samples associated with the lead program. General Chemistry Supervisor is the Record Retention Coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 12 years. ; Electronic: Keep 12 years.

series_description: The (material) safety data sheets for chemicals and reagents used in the Consumer Protection Laboratory. Record Retention Coordinator is the admin laboratory technicians.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 30 years after obsolete. ; Electronic: Keep for 30 years after obsolete.

series_description: This includes records from any vendor that transports and disposes hazardous chemical waste (Clean Harbors) generated by the Consumer Protection Lab (CPL) and the Analytical Toxicology Lab (ATL) Bio-pickup. Keeping these records for 5 years is a U.S. Environmental Protection Agency requirement. The Chemical Hygiene Officer is the Record Retention Coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 5 years.

series_title: Analytical Toxicology Lab (ATL) Routine Lab Records
series_description: Routine Lab Records including but not limited to Accessioning Paperwork, Batch Paperwork, Chain of Custody Paperwork, and Cleared Folders. The Analytical Toxicology Laboratory (ATL) Administrative Professional 2 is the Record Retention Coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 2 years. ; Electronic: Keep for 2 years.

series_title: Analytical Toxicology Lab (ATL) Master Case Files
series_description: Including but not limited to accessions, instrumental, and method data, USA LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System) accession and reporting info, and Quality & Supportive Records (including Proficiency Testing Paperwork, obsolete equipment manuals and service records, temperature records, room access records, certificate of analysis, standard verification, calibration records, instrument tunes and calibrations). The Analytical Toxicology Lab (ATL) Administrative Professional 2 is the Record Retention Coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years ; Electronic: 5 years

series_description: All records associated with the Quality Management System including but not limited to: Obsolete Quality Manual (Tier I), Obsolete Procedures (Tier II and III), Audits (Internal and External), Management Reviews, Proficiency Test Results, Meeting Minutes (Quality Assurance Meetings, Quality Assurance Unit Meetings, Supervisor and Section), Corrective Action Reports (CARs), Preventive Action Reports (PARs), Improvement Action Reports (IARs), Vendor Evaluations, Complaints, Customer Surveys. The Quality Assurance Officer (QAO) is the Record Retention Coordinator.

series_title: Analytical Toxicology Lab Archived Standard Operating Procedures and Method Validation Records
series_description: Analytical Toxicology Lab (ATL) archived Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) are procedures that will no longer be used in the Analytical Toxicology Lab (ATL). Method Validation records are records that the lab created when validating the procedure. The Quality Assurance Coordinator is the Record Retention Coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy 10 years after the SOP has been archived. ; Electronic: Destroy 10 years after the SOP has been archived.

series_title: Analytical Toxicoloy Lab (ATL) Obsolete Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)
series_description: Obsolete Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) are defined as SOPs that have been revised and have been replaced by a new revision. The obsolete revision is retained for reference purposes. The Quality Assurance Coordinator is the record retention coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years ; Electronic: 5 years

series_description: All records associated with training which include external documents related to position descriptions. The Section Supervisor is the Record Retention Coordinator.

series_title: Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act (FIFRA) Laboratory Records
series_description: Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act (FIFRA) laboratory records include laboratory analytical reports, sample collection reports, e-mails communicating sample testing, laboratory Quality Assurance/Quality Control data (spikes, blanks, recovery etc), all other data generated by the laboratory. The FIFRA supervisor is the Record Retention coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years ; Electronic: 5 years

series_description: Pesticide Data Program (PDP)- laboratory supporting records which include laboratory logs/forms/logbooks, Certificate of Analysis (COA''s), related records pertinent to evaluation/interpretation of data, and data packages not specific to PDP method validation. The PDP Supervisor is the Record Retention Coordinator.

The Laboratory Section Supervisors are the Record Retention Coordinators.

retention_statement: Paper: 5 years ; Electronic: 5 years

series_description: Equipment Manuals and Service Records. The Section Supervisors are the Record Retention Coordinators.
retention_statement: Paper: Life of Equipment + 3 years ; Electronic: Life of Equipment + 3 years

series_description: All records associated with legal cases. The Section Supervisor of the legal case is the Record Retention Coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years ; Electronic: 5 years

series_title: Safety Data Sheets (SDS) formerly Material Safety Data Sheets (MSDS)
series_description: Manufacturer specifications for safe handling of hazardous chemicals or biologics. These records provide information about the ingredients of products, handling requirements, first aid and clean up directions. The Safety Officer is the Record Retention Coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 30 years after obsolete. ; Electronic: Keep for 30 years after obsolete.

series_description: All records associated with the Quality Management System including but not limited to: Obsolete Quality Manual (Tier I), Obsolete Procedures (Tier II and III), Audits (Internal and External), Management Reviews, Proficiency Test Results, Meeting Minutes (Quality Assurance Meeting (QAM), Quality Assurance Unit Meeting (QAUM), Supervisor and Section), Corrective Action Reports (CARs), Preventive Action Reports (PARs), Improvement Action Reports (IARs), Vendor Evaluations, Complaints, Customer Surveys. series_description: All laboratory records associated with Federal Emergency Response Network (FERN) FDA (Food and Drug Administration) and FERN FSIS(Food Safety and Inspection Service) cooperative agreement programs. This includes the grant, the cooperative agreement contract and the award. The Section Supervisor is the Record Retention Coordinator for the laboratory records and the Financial Associate is the Record Retention Coordinator for the grant, contract and award.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 3 years after the Cooperative Agreement has ended (after the final expenditure report). ; Electronic: Retain for 3 years after the Cooperative Agreement has ended (after the final expenditure report).

series_description: Equipment Manual and Service Records. The Section Supervisor is the Record Retention Coordinator.
retention_statement: Paper: Life of Equipment + 3 years ; Electronic: Life of Equipment + 3 years

series_description: Documentation certifying the destruction or transfer of records according o approved records retention schedules. May include the title of the record series, inclusive dates, retention schedule number, volume of records and method of disposal. These records will be saved electronically on the CPL shared drive.
retention_statement: Electronic: Permanently or until of no legal value. ; Paper: Permanently or until of no legal value.

series_description: USALIMS (Laboratory Information Management System) Reports, Sample Submission Forms and any other pertinent information submitted by the client (Not specified otherwise by a specific program). The record retention coordinator for hard copy sample submission forms is the Receiving Laboratory Technician.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 years ; Electronic: 3 years

series_description: Reports that document what Plant Pest Control personnel have completed on a weekly and monthly basis i.e. (weekly itineraries, monthly reports, field timesheets.)
retention_statement: Electronic: The originals are scanned into the PPC drive and will be retained for 3 years electronically. ; Paper: The original document will be scanned and the paper copy will be retained until the document is verified readable in our PPC drive; once the paper copy is verified readable it will be put in the waste paper.

series_description: Licensure regarding dealer’s ie (Lowes , Meijer, and landscaping firms) who have bought plants from nurseries and are re-selling. Encompasses all documents needed to be licensed, inspected, and reporting i.e. (application, inspection reports, reports.)
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year after final expiration, including all renewals, or 1 year after denial. ; Electronic: Keep 1 year after final expiration, including all renewals, or 1 year after denial.

series_description: Plants, lumber, grain, seeds, and agricultural products shipped out of the state of Ohio to another state or country are inspected by Plant Pest Control Personnel and receive certification for shipment.
retention_statement: Paper: The originals are scanned into the PPC drive and will be retained for 3 years electronically. ; Electronic: All Phytosanitary Certificates are completed electronically and stored electronically indefinitely in a system called PCIT administered by USDA / APHIS. They will be retained indefinitely per PCIT system.

series_description: Nurseries are inspected yearly by Plant Pest Control Personnel. If the inspection does not reveal any disease or insect problems and the stock is able to be sold, the Nursery is issued a Certificate. Copies of all supporting documentation regarding the inspection, findings, and certification are kept in the division’s Nursery Files i.e. (applications, inspection reports, rejection notices, and restrictions)
retention_statement: Paper: The original document will be scanned and the paper copy will be retained until the document is verified readable in our PPC drive; once the paper copy is verified readable it will be put in the waste paper. ; Electronic: The originals are scanned into the PPC drive and will be retained for 3 years electronically.

series_description: These are tests on plants, insects, and bees; samples are obtained and submitted by Plant Pest Control and Apiary Inspectors. The tests are analyzed in the Plant Pest Division or outside contracted labs i.e (microscopic examination of sample.) Tests results are recorded in a corresponding division report.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep 1 year, if the report contains no items of interest. Keep 3 years if the report contains information on a disease or insect problem ; Electronic: Keep 1 year, if the report contains no items of interest. Keep 3 years if the report contains information on a disease or insect problem

series_description: This is a licensing program for individuals and businesses that own or sell honey bees. Apiaries and Queens are licensed and inspected. All supporting documentation regarding the inspection, findings, and licensure are kept in the division’s Apiary Files but not limited to i.e. ( applications, correspondence, licenses, inspection reports, queen apiary rearing certificates, expense reports, etc.)
retention_statement: Paper: Three years ; Electronic: Three years

series_title: Archived Standard Operating Procedures and Method Validation Records
series_description: Archived Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) are procedures of methods that will no longer be used in the lab. Method Validation Records are records that the laboratory produced when validating the procedure. (Not specified otherwise by a specific program). The Quality Assurance Officer is the record retention coordinator for the archived SOPs and the Lab Supervisor for the Method Validation Records.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 5 years after the SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) has been archived. ; Electronic: Keep for 5 years after the SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) has been archived.

series_description: Consumer Protection Lab (CPL) Basement Record Room access log. The record retention coordinator is the Receiving Laboratory Technician.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 years ; Electronic: 3 years

This includes the grant, the cooperative agreement contract and the award. Retain Records 7 years after the cooperative agreement program has ended. The Supervisor of the cooperative agreement is the record retention coordinator.

series_description: All grant records concerning Federal grants to Plant Pest Control Division including grant application forms, financial status reports, expenditures, and employee hours tracked against the work plan.

series_description: Inspections in which a business or individuals must comply with Plant Pest Control regulations that may result in the destruction, seizure, restriction, or transport of plants, lumber, grain, seed, and agricultural products. Reports or Forms that may accompany Nursery Stock Rejection Notice, Regulated Article Seizure, Restriction / Seizure Release, or Un-licensed location investigation, and compliance agreements.
retention_statement: Paper: The original document will be scanned and the paper copy will be retained until the document is verified readable in our PPC drive; once the paper copy is verified readable it will be put in the waste paper. ; Electronic: The originals are scanned into the PPC drive and will be retained for 3 years electronically.

series_title: Pest Survey Data Forms and Gypsy Moth Trapper Data Downloads
series_description: Plant Pest Control Division uses GPS, G3, GIS software to track, capture, store, manipulate, analyze, manage, and present spatial or geographic data to track locations of invasive insects, create maps, and track locations of invasive insect traps throughout the State of Ohio. The data is stored within Plant Pest Control and G3 is archived by Virginia Tech indefinitely.
retention_statement: Electronic: The originals are scanned into the PPC drive and will be retained for 3 years electronically. ; Paper: The original document will be scanned and the paper copy will be retained until the document is verified readable in our PPC drive; once the paper copy is verified readable it will be put in the waste paper.

series_title: Archived Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and Method Validation Records
series_description: Archived Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) are procedures of methods that will no longer be used in the lab. Method Validation Records are records that the laboratory produced when validating the procedure. (Not specified otherwise by a specific program). Quality Assurance Officer is the Record Retention Coordinator for the Archived SOPs and the Lab Supervisor is the Record Retention Coordinator for the Method Validation Records.
retention_statement: Electronic: Destroy 7 years after the SOP has been archived. ; Paper: Destroy 7 years after the SOP has been archived.

series_description: Pesticide Data Program (PDP) Financial Records: Each State is required to retain financial records including supporting documentation needed to substantiate charges made to PDP. This includes the grant, the cooperative agreement contract and the award.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 3 years and/or audited following the date of submission of the final expenditure report or until transferred to machine readable format. ; Electronic: Retain for 3 years and/or audited following the date of submission of the final expenditure report.

series_description: This is a license for entities that receive producer milk and are responsible for paying the producer. (This excludes producer marketing organizations). Dealers buy milk and process it. The license ensures that the buyer of the milk can pay the producer. Our file on the dealer includes financial statements (confidential information) evaluated when a license is issued. The license is perpetual.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep for 4 years after a license is inactive. A license is active until the license holder goes out of business or the license is revoked. ; Paper: Keep for 4 years after a license is inactive. A license is active until the license holder goes out of business or the license is revoked.

series_description: This is a license issued to a business or individual that owns, leases, or controls a milk tank truck or conveyance. The license is perpetual and remains active until the hauler goes out of business or the license is revoked.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 4 years after the license is inactive. ; Electronic: Keep for 4 years after the license is inactive.

series_description: This series contains Payment Detail and Revenue Receipt information for transactions conducted with the Dairy Division. Payment records would include any and all payments for services or goods purchased by the division. Revenue records would include incoming payments to the division for license fees and milk inspection fee invoices.
retention_statement: Paper: 2 years after the year in which the event occurred or until audited.

series_description: This is a license issued to a milk producer i.e. a dairy farmer. Licenses are perpetual, and they are active as long as the producer is in business and the license has not been revoked.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive. ; Electronic: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive.

series_description: This record is a license for businesses that process and package milk or milk products and businesses that are licensed under the Ohio Frozen Dessert Rules (Chapter 901:11-3). This license is perpetual.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive. ; Electronic: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive.

series_description: This record is a license allowing an individual to weigh, sample, and test milk. Department of Agriculture inspectors administer the test for the license. These licenses are perpetual and remain active as long as the individual is in business and the license has not been revoked.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive. ; Paper: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive.

series_title: Milk Inspection Fee and Milk Hauler Invoices
series_description: This is an invoice sent by the Dairy Division detailing milk inspection fees and milk hauler fees. Invoices sent back to ODA with payments are kept to research billing disputes.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the invoices were sent.

series_description: This guide shows the details of a milk producer installation in regards to the equipment and facility layout.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive. ; Paper: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive.

series_description: This guide shows the details of a milk processor installation in regards to the equipment and facility layout.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive. ; Electronic: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive.

series_description: These letters identify specify locations on milk contact surfaces for licensed dairy producers, dairy processors and milk tankers that have been chemically burned, but pose no risk to milk quality.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive. ; Electronic: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive.

series_description: These letters advise a licensee, which includes Milk Dealers, Milk Haulers, Milk Processors, Milk Producers and Weigher, Sampler or Testers, of violations of the Ohio Administrative Code section 901:11
retention_statement: Paper: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive. ; Electronic: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive.

series_description: These inspections detail the results of sanitation inspections of Milk Haulers, Milk Processors, Milk Producers and Weigher, Sampler or Testers. These reports show specifically what conditions are in violation of the Ohio Administrative Code section 901:11.
retention_statement: Electronic: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive. ; Paper: Keep for 4 years after the year in which the license went inactive.
